tool_intro_blurb: Test values, represent solution sets, and justify boundaries with algebraic and graphical evidence.
hint_type_2d: Type a two-variable inequality like y > 2x + 1 to graph on the Cartesian plane.
hint_type_1d: Type an inequality like x > 3 or a compound like -2 < x ≤ 5 to visualize it on a number line.
hero_1d_hint: Open circle for < and >, closed for ≤ and ≥. Compound inequalities (-2 < x ≤ 5) shade the band BETWEEN the two endpoints. Common Core 6.EE.8, 7.EE.4.
hero_2d_hint: Solid line for ≤/≥, dashed for </>. Test point (0,0) tells you which side to shade. Multiple constraints overlap into a feasible region — the foundation of linear programming.
test_discovery_desc: Sliders for x, coefficient, bound. Test whether coef·x < bound. 3 discrete states. No score, no reveal.
hypothesis_placeholder: Hypothesis: When does a negative coefficient flip the inequality?
prompt_test_x5: Test x = 5 with coef = -2 and bound = 5. What happens?
prompt_why_flip: Why does multiplying by negative flip < to >?
understand_explain: I understand — explain in own words
design_note_test: Design note: discrete 3-state test marker; no answer reveal — by design.
